text:
Once upon a time in a quiet town nestled between whispering woods and a sparkling creek lived a curious squirrel. His name was Olive.

One glorious autumn day, Olive decided she wanted more than nuts. She scaled the nearest elm for a peculiar object hanging there—a bell. Her heart skipped a beat when she heard a tinkling sound. She touched it eagerly.

Oh!

A secret door!

With twinkling eyes, Olive scampered inside. An old clock ticked somewhere ahead. Each cuckoo that sounded seemed to guide her farther, deeper.

At last she reached an old oak tree with hanging roses. The door creaked as she pushed it open.

Inside was time itself—a library brimming with timepieces. And at its heart sat a wise old clockmaker.

"Lost," the clockmaker pensively said.

Olive looked around. Everything was old.

"You've wandered far," continued the clockmaker. "...But these clocks hold tales."

She listened. One ticked for years gone
